title: Fixing CSS Custom Property Inheritance with Variable Number of Children

statement.markdown: Write a function that fixes a CSS class to correctly inherit custom properties from its parent when it has a variable number of children. The CSS class is represented as a JavaScript object where each key is a CSS property and its value is another object with 'parent' and 'children' properties. The custom property should be inherited from the parent to all children. The function should return the updated CSS class.
statement.inputFormat: An object representing the CSS class.
statement.notes: The custom property should be inherited from the parent to all children. If a child has its own custom property, it should override the inherited property.

constraints.description: Number of children in the CSS class

examples.explanation: The custom property is inherited from the parent to the child.
The custom property is not overridden by the child that inherits it.

buggyStarterCode.javascript: function fixCustomProperty(cssClass) { for (let child of cssClass.children) { if (child.customProperty === 'inherit') { child.customProperty = 'override'; } } return cssClass; }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def fix_custom_property(self, css_class) { for child in css_class.children: if child.custom_property == 'inherit': child.custom_property = 'override' return css_class;

solutions.python: class Solution: def fix_custom_property(self, css_class) { for child in css_class.children: if child.custom_property is None or child.custom_property === 'inherit': child.custom_property = css_class.parent.custom_property; return css_class;
